title: HEXPIRETIME
description: Returns the expiration time of a hash field as a Unix timestamp, in seconds.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Returns the absolute Unix timestamp in seconds since Unix epoch at which the given key's field(s) will expire. See also the [`HPEXPIRETIME`]() command, which returns the same information with millisecond resolution. ## Examples ``` redis> HSET mykey field1 "hello" field2 "world" (integer) 2 redis> HEXPIRE mykey 300 FIELDS 2 field1 field2 1) (integer) 1 2) (integer) 1 redis> HEXPIRETIME mykey FIELDS 2 field1 field2 1) (integer) 1715705914 2) (integer) 1715705914 ``` ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays). For each field: - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-2` if no such field exists in the provided hash key, or the provided key does not exist. - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-1` if the field exists but has no associated expiration set. - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the expiration (Unix timestamp) in seconds. **RESP3:** * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays). For each field: - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-2` if no such field exists in the provided hash key, or the provided key does not exist. - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-1` if the field exists but has no associated expiration set. - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the expiration (Unix timestamp) in seconds.
intro:
Returns the absolute Unix timestamp in seconds since Unix epoch at which the given key's field(s) will expire.

See also the [`HPEXPIRETIME`]() command, which returns the same information with millisecond resolution.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns the absolute Unix timestamp in seconds since Unix epoch at which the given key's field(s) will expire.

See also the [`HPEXPIRETIME`]() command, which returns the same information with millisecond resolution.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
```
redis> HSET mykey field1 "hello" field2 "world"
(integer) 2
redis> HEXPIRE mykey 300  FIELDS 2 field1 field2
1) (integer) 1
2) (integer) 1
redis> HEXPIRETIME mykey FIELDS 2 field1 field2
1) (integer) 1715705914
2) (integer) 1715705914
```

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays). For each field:
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-2` if no such field exists in the provided hash key, or the provided key does not exist.
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-1` if the field exists but has no associated expiration set.
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the expiration (Unix timestamp) in seconds.

**RESP3:**

* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays). For each field:
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-2` if no such field exists in the provided hash key, or the provided key does not exist.
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `-1` if the field exists but has no associated expiration set.
    - [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the expiration (Unix timestamp) in seconds.
